title: Delphinium araraticum
intro: Delphinium araraticum, also known as Armenian cobalt blue larkspur, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus and western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Delphinium araraticum include delphiniemine, delphineine, and delphineine alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
